commit.message:
fix: clear all checkpoint times for runner, not just one checkpoint

clearCheckpointRunner now queries all checkpoint_runners records for
the runner and resets every one back to not-started with null times.
This fixes the runner remaining as 'Active' after a clear (the grouped
status was still Active because other checkpoint records were untouched).

Button label updated to 'Clear All Times' to reflect the behaviour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add two missing Recent Changes entries to .github/copilot-instructions.md:

- Update Diff Display Enhancement (PR #2834): --yes/-y flag as alias
  for --force; enhanced diff listing with old → new values + emoji icons
- Visual Before/After Diff Table (PR #2858): displayChangesSummary()
  renders a 4-column Component/Before/After/Impact table ordered by
  severity (recreate-required → reboot-required → in-place)

These entries were present in user-facing docs (faq.md, CLI flags) but
missing from the copilot instructions used by AI code assistants.

commit.message:
fix: improve wake phrase accuracy with --prompt hint and fuzzy matching

- Pass the wake phrase as --prompt to whisper-cli so it biases transcription
  toward 'Copilot' instead of phonetic guesses like 'coballot' or 'co lot'.
- Add Levenshtein fuzzy matching fallback: each word in the phrase is matched
  against transcribed words within ~25% edit distance, so slight mishearings
  still trigger activation even if --prompt isn't enough.

commit.message:
feat: add Clear Time action per runner/checkpoint in base station

- Add clearCheckpointRunner(runnerNumber, checkpointNumber) store action
  that reverts a runner at a checkpoint back to not-started with null times
- Add 'Clear Time' button to RunnerTimeEditModal (shown only when a time
  already exists) via new onClear prop
- Wire onClear in HeadsUpGrid using the new store action
- Fix broken existingTime/isDuplicate lookups in HeadsUpGrid (were
  checking r.checkpointNumber which doesn't exist on the grouped runner
  objects; now uses r.checkpointTimes[cpNumber] correctly)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: macOS native build uses Mac Catalyst target for UIKit

The macos-native job failed because xnuimagefuzzer.m imports UIKit
(87 API references), which is not available in the raw macOS SDK.

Changes:
- Use -target arm64-apple-ios17.2-macabi (Mac Catalyst clang target)
- Add -iframework for iOSSupport frameworks path
- Compile all 4 source files (was missing AppDelegate/SceneDelegate/ViewController)
- Link UIKit instead of AppKit
- Match build-native.sh flags for consistency

This keeps the Job 2 purpose (validate clang build without xcodebuild)
while using the correct target triple for UIKit-dependent code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve three wrapper generation bugs found via stress testing

1. Scan function parameter/return types for opaque struct references
   Types like sqlite3_blob only appear in function signatures (not struct
   members) and were missing forward declarations, causing UndefVarError
   at module load time.

2. Exclude enum names from forward declarations
   Enum types (e.g., http_method) defined via @enum were getting duplicate
   empty struct forward declarations that shadowed the enum definition.

3. Sanitize union accessor type names and defer emission
   - Union member type names now go through _sanitize_julia_type_name()
     to match actual struct definitions (e.g., __pthread_mutex_s → _pthread_mutex_s)
   - Unknown Ptr{X} inner types fall back to Ptr{Cvoid}
   - Union accessor functions are deferred to after all struct definitions
     to avoid forward reference errors

Validated against: SQLite (269 functions), cJSON (79 functions),
tinyexpr (7 functions), http-parser (15 functions with enums),
Duktape (JS engine), and the existing test suite (81 tests pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Transactions (2), GST (2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CFL-073: icMBBFromXml channel count validation (CWE-122)

Root cause: icMBBFromXml() passes full int nIn/nOut from XML attributes
to icCurvesFromXml(), but NewCurvesA/B/M() allocates using icUInt8Number
(truncates to 0-255). With OutputChannels="6914", allocation =
6914 & 0xFF = 2 pointers (16 bytes), but loop iterates up to 6914.

Fix: Validate nIn/nOut in range [1,16] before Init(). ICC profiles
support max 16 channels per color space; values outside this range
indicate malformed XML.

Both iccFromXml tool AND icc_fromxml_fuzzer crash identically — this
is a valid upstream bug, not a fidelity gap.

Crash: heap-buffer-overflow in icCurvesFromXml() at IccTagXml.cpp:3330
Trigger: crash-a39b6ce2b74a007bc4096bf1b49589f0a3bbc23d (4541B XML)
CWE-122: Heap-based Buffer Overflow via integer truncation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: reduce EncodeMessage complexity from CC=17 to CC=3

Extract argument encoding logic into two helper functions:
- encodeArguments: loops over arguments and collects file descriptors
- encodeArgument: handles individual argument type marshaling

This decomposition reduces cyclomatic complexity by 82.4% while
maintaining identical behavior. All tests pass.

Addresses audit finding: Wire protocol encoding function has high
cyclomatic complexity (AUDIT-2026-03-07.md).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: update Homebrew formula to v1.0.20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

masternode: Fix gossip self-reporting so direct peers count themselves as reporters

The gossip broadcaster called broadcast_status_gossip() to tell OTHER nodes
'I see these masternodes', but never processed the gossip locally. So node A's
own peer_reports for masternodes it was directly connected to was always empty.
60 seconds later, cleanup_stale_reports() would see 0 reports and flip is_active
to false -- even though A has a live TCP connection to that node.

Fix 1: Call process_status_gossip(self, visible, now) before broadcasting so
the local registry records our own sightings. Now A's peer_reports['F'] = {'A'}
right away, and when B also gossips 'I see F', A gets {'A','B'} = 2 reporters.

Fix 2: Lower min_reports threshold from 3 to 2 for networks with 5-12
masternodes (testnet range). Leaf nodes in pyramid topology connect upward to
5-6 nodes; having 2 reporters is sufficient proof of liveness in this range.
Large networks (13+) still require 3 reporters for Sybil resistance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Precompile markdown parser regexes to avoid per-call construction (#1070)

* Precompile markdown parser regexes for improved performance

The Punctuation and HtmlEntity active patterns in MarkdownInlineParser.fs
were constructing regex pattern strings and calling Regex.Match on each
invocation. The Punctuation pattern is called for every character during
inline parsing, making the repeated regex construction a hot path.

Changes:
- Extract punctuationRegex and htmlEntityRegex as module-level compiled
  Regex values (RegexOptions.Compiled) in MarkdownInlineParser.fs
- Limit the input string passed to each regex to its theoretical maximum
  match length (2 chars for punctuation, 34 chars for entities), avoiding
  conversion of the entire remaining input list on every call
- Extract blockquoteRegex as a module-level compiled Regex in
  MarkdownBlockParser.fs, removing the per-call string concatenation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

---------

Co-authored-by: Repo Assist <repo-assist@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
benchmark: Display deltas as percentages with 2 decimal points

Changed from 'Nx faster' to '+XX.XX% faster/slower' format for clearer comparison. The percentage format makes it easier to understand the magnitude of performance differences, especially when comparing close results.

Example: '4.05% slower' is more intuitive than '1.1× slower'.

commit.message:
refactor: decompose high-complexity layout functions

Extract shared logic from layoutRow and layoutColumn into helper functions:
- computeFlexMeasurements: extracts flex metrics for children
- distributeFlex: computes final sizes based on flex rules
- computeJustifyOffset: calculates starting offset for justification
- computeCrossAlign: computes cross-axis position and size

Complexity reduction:
- layoutRow: CC 17 → 3 (82.4% improvement), 107 → 26 lines
- layoutColumn: CC 17 → 3 (82.4% improvement), 107 → 26 lines
- Duplication ratio: 2.40% → 2.07%
- All tests pass, documentation coverage maintained at 98.8%

Fixes audit finding AUDIT-2026-03-07.md (MEDIUM severity)

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add ApiDocParameter and ApiDocReturnInfo named record types (#1066)

* Add ApiDocParameter and ApiDocReturnInfo named record types for cross-assembly usability

Replace anonymous records in ApiDocMember.Parameters and ApiDocMember.ReturnInfo
with named record types ApiDocParameter and ApiDocReturnInfo. Anonymous records
from different assemblies are not structurally compatible, making these properties
difficult to consume externally.

All field names are preserved, so existing code using .ParameterNameText,
.ParameterType, .ParameterDocs, .ReturnType, .ReturnDocs continues to work
without modification.

Closes #735

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

programmatically' section of docs/apidocs.fsx, showing how to access
ApiDocMember.Parameters (ApiDocParameter list) and ApiDocMember.ReturnInfo
(ApiDocReturnInfo) and how to pass them to user-defined functions.

commit.message:
Feat/tidy up options (#17)

* feat: CLI simplification Phase 1 & 2 - Remove deprecated flags and rename options

Phase 1 & 2 of CLI simplification as approved:

Phase 1 Changes (v1.2):
- Added deprecation warnings for --format and --all flags
- Updated help text to show new --target and --skip-gitignore options
- Added category aliases documentation (aws→aws-cloud, azure→azure-cloud, gamedev→game-dev)
- All changes backward compatible; old flags still work with deprecation warnings

Phase 2 Changes (v2.0):
- BREAKING: Removed --format flag entirely
- BREAKING: Removed --all flag (use --target mixed instead)
- BREAKING: Renamed --no-gitignore to --skip-gitignore
- Updated parseInstallTarget() to throw helpful error messages for removed flags
- Updated shell completions (bash, zsh, fish) for new syntax
- Updated all documentation and examples to use new syntax
- Updated help text to remove deprecated options section

Migration Path:
- --format copilot → --target copilot
- --format claude → --target claude
- --format cursor → --target cursor
- --all → --target mixed
- --no-gitignore → --skip-gitignore

Complexity Reduction:
- Removed 2 flags (--format, --all)
- Renamed 1 flag (--no-gitignore)
- Reduced CLI complexity from 6.5/10 to 4.5/10
- Simplified flag naming: all negative flags now use --skip- prefix

All 63 tests passing. Complete backward compatibility removed in Phase 2 to
force migration to cleaner, more intuitive syntax.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Target-specific directory installation for skills, prompts, and agents

Changed install behavior to place skills and prompts in target-specific directories
instead of always using the .agent/ folder. This ensures proper auto-discovery by each tool.

Directory Structure by Target:

COPILOT (--target copilot):
  .github/copilot-instructions.md - Main instruction file
  .github/skills/ - Skills (installed and indexed)
  .github/prompts/ - Prompts (installed)
  .github/agents/ - Agent instructions (installed)

CLAUDE (--target claude):
  CLAUDE.md - Main instruction file (composed from skills + agents)
  Skills and prompts composed into CLAUDE.md, NOT installed to disk

CURSOR (--target cursor):
  .cursorrules - Main instruction file (composed from skills + agents)
  Skills and prompts composed into .cursorrules, NOT installed to disk

MIXED (--target mixed):
  .github/copilot-instructions.md - Copilot file
  CLAUDE.md - Claude file (composed)
  .cursorrules - Cursor file (composed)
  .github/skills/ - Skills (installed for Copilot primary)
  .github/prompts/ - Prompts (installed for Copilot primary)
  .github/agents/ - Agents (installed for Copilot primary)

Changes:
- Added resolveContentOutputDirs() function to resolve target-specific directories
- Updated cmdInstall() to use target-specific paths instead of manifest.outputDir
- Added --target option to agent diff command
- Removed dependency on .agent/ folder for Copilot installations
- All 63 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: redesign error pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: solar panel model scale bugs — ear-clip triangulation, Shoelace closing edge, area source

- Fix incomplete Shoelace formula in _ensure_ccw_xy: add closing edge
  (x[-1]*z[0] - x[0]*z[-1]) so winding check works for off-origin polygons

- Replace naive fan triangulation with ear-clipping algorithm in
  _region_to_mesh: fan triangulation inflates mesh area for concave
  roof polygons (L-shape, U-shape, T-shape), causing frontend
  _calibrateSceneUnitsForRoof to compute an oversized scale factor
  and panels to render too large

- Fix inputs.js: use roof.area (actual polygon area) instead of
  roof.panel_area (inscribed rectangle area) for ROI area display

- Remove dead panel_parts code from build_glb_from_roofs: panel meshes
  were computed but never exported to the GLB scene

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
